vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #1320

plan.title: framework:check-updates probes consumer origin (statusreport.git) on vendored installs; never detects framework drift

narratives.Description: framework:check-updates probes consumer origin (statusreport.git) on vendored installs; never detects framework drift
narratives.Overview:
## Summary

`task deft:framework:check-updates` (`cmd_check_updates` in `run`, #801 probe) resolves the wrong upstream URL on **vendored, version-pinned installs** (`.deft/core/` deposit with `.deft/VERSION` pin, no submodule). It points at the consumer project's own `origin` remote instead of the framework upstream (`deftai/directive`), so the gate silently returns `no-tags` and never warns even when the consumer is multiple releases behind.

## Reproduction

Consumer project: `deftai/statusreport` at branch `deftai-upgrade-20260518`.

Install style:

- `./.deft/core/` (vendored framework, no `.git` inside)
- `./.deft/VERSION` pins `v0.31.0` (sha `d4257b4ba8594f06ec8802a1e5c2560d17cb833c`, `fetched_at: 2026-05-18T22:23:29.387Z`, `fetched_by: 'DEFT.directive[bot]'`)
- No `.gitmodules` at the project root
- No `./deft/` legacy submodule directory

Run:

```
task deft:framework:check-updates -- --json --force
```

Returns:

```json
{
  "current": "0.0.0-dev",
  "status": "no-tags",
  "upstream_url": "https://github.com/deftai/statusreport.git"
}
```

`upstream_url` is the consumer project's `origin` (statusreport), not the framework upstream. `current: "0.0.0-dev"` is also suspicious — the consumer's `.deft/VERSION` clearly records `v0.31.0`.

Direct query against the real framework upstream:

```
gh release list --repo deftai/directive --limit 5
# v0.33.0 (latest, ~33 min ago)
# v0.32.1 (~2 days ago)
# v0.32.0 (~2 days ago)
# v0.31.0 (~5 days ago)
# v0.30.0 (~9 days ago)
```

So the consumer is **3 releases behind** but the probe silently reports `no-tags` and exit 0.

## Expected behavior

For a vendored install:

- `upstream_url` should resolve to the framework upstream (`https://github.com/deftai/directive.git`) — most reliably read from `.deft/VERSION`'s provenance manifest, an explicit project-config knob, or a baked-in framework constant.
- `current` should read the framework version from `.deft/VERSION` (or the `.deft-version` marker), not the in-process `VERSION` constant of whatever framework lives on disk (which defaults to `0.0.0-dev` in some build contexts).
- With `current=v0.31.0` and upstream tags `v0.32.0 / v0.32.1 / v0.33.0` present, status should be `behind` with exit code `1`.

## Hypothesis

`cmd_check_updates` calls `_run_remote_probe(project_root)`, which appears to look for a `./deft` submodule and fall back to the consumer project's `origin` when the submodule isn't present. For the v0.20+ canonical install layout (`.deft/core/` + `.deft/VERSION`), this fallback is incorrect — the framework's true upstream is recorded in the YAML provenance manifest at `.deft/core/VERSION`, not derivable from the consumer's `origin` (which by definition is the *consumer's* repo, not the framework's).

## Impact

The periodic remote-drift gate (#801) is effectively dead for vendored installs, which is now the canonical install layout per AGENTS.md template v3. Consumers fall arbitrarily far behind without any session-start warning. The 24-hour throttle and `vbrief/.deft-remote-probe.json` state file are no defense — the probe affirmatively returns `no-tags` rather than erroring.

## Suggested fix

Resolve `upstream_url` in this priority order:

1. Parse the YAML manifest at `<install>/VERSION` for a `source.url` (or similar) field.
2. Read an explicit `framework.upstream_url` knob from `vbrief/PROJECT-DEFINITION.vbrief.json` or `.deft/config.toml`.
3. Use a baked-in framework constant (`DEFT_UPSTREAM_URL = "https://github.com/deftai/directive.git"`).
4. **Never** fall back to the consumer's `origin` — that is guaranteed wrong on a vendored install.

Also resolve `current` by reading `.deft/VERSION` (`ref` or `tag` field) before falling back to the in-process `VERSION`.

## Environment

- Consumer: `deftai/statusreport`, branch `deftai-upgrade-20260518`
- Framework on disk: v0.31.0 (sha `d4257b4`)
- Latest framework: v0.33.0
- OS: Windows 11, pwsh 7.6.1
- `gh` CLI 2.x (authenticated)

## Related

- #801 (remote-version probe)
- #1046 (install-manifest / install-path)
- #1062 (install-root field in manifest)

references.title: Issue #1320: framework:check-updates probes consumer origin (statusreport.git) on vendored installs; never detects framework drift
